requirements.title: OAuth flow surfaced to host terminal ŌĆö `pnpm gh:auth` invokes `gh auth login` inside the container and the OAuth URL is surfaced to the host terminal; completing the flow in a host browser returns control to the CLI
substrate_evidence: packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-237 NEW; iter-239 retrofit 82 lines; 0755): `#!/usr/bin/env bash` + `set -euo pipefail` + `unset COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME` (Story 2.6 AI-8/AI-12 + Story 2.7 SC-10 + Story 2.8 SC-8 defensive-posture precedent preserved). `CONTAINER_NAME="${KEEL_DEVBOX_CONTAINER_NAME:-keel-devbox}"` (Story 2.2 .envrc parameterisation consumed). `SCRIPT_DIR` at line 39 for iter-239 `source lib/main-repo-resolver.sh` + `source lib/check-mount-source.sh` retrofits (diverges from Story 2.8 claude-host.sh no-SCRIPT_DIR posture; shared-lib sourcing purpose, NOT start.sh sub-invoke ŌĆö SC-4 no-auto-start contract preserved). Banner cites Story 2.9 + AC 1ŌĆō4 + Story 2.6 `<verb>-host.sh` pattern + Story 2.7 + Story 2.8 inheritance + exit-code contract (including novel exit 12 per iter-239).

packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-237 NEW): Pre-flight 1 ŌĆö `docker info --format '{{.ServerVersion}}' >/dev/null 2>&1 || { log "docker unreachable ŌĆö is the daemon running?"; exit 8; }`. Inherits Story 2.7 `ralph-build-host.sh:36` + Story 2.8 `claude-host.sh` tighter variant (catches reachable-but-broken-daemon states).

packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-237 NEW): Pre-flight 2 ŌĆö container state inspect ŌåÆ exit 9 if not running with `'pnpm devbox:start' first` hint. SC-4 no-auto-start posture ŌĆö auth is a one-off gesture, not a loop-entry gesture. Mirrors Story 2.8 posture.

packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-239 RETROFIT): Pre-flight 3 ŌĆö `check_mount_source` sourced from `lib/check-mount-source.sh`; exits 12 on bind-mount source mismatch with a `pnpm devbox:restart` hint. Orthogonal to AC 1 OAuth-flow envelope ŌĆö defends against worktree-A-then-worktree-B bind-source-stale race.

packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-237 NEW; iter-239 retrofit): Final exec ŌĆö `exec docker exec -it --user dev -w "${CONTAINER_WORKDIR}" -e "KEEL_DEVBOX_REPO_NAME=${REPO_NAME}" "${CONTAINER_NAME}" gh auth login "$@"`. Interactive-only; hardcoded `-it` (no TTY-detect gate per SC-6 ŌĆö OAuth flow IS the operator-interactive AC; same posture as Story 2.7 SC-8 + Story 2.8 SC-6). `--user dev` binds to UID 1000 per Story 2.5 hardening substrate. `-w ${CONTAINER_WORKDIR}` post-iter-239 parameterised (was hardcoded `/workspace` at iter-237 landing). `gh auth login` hardcoded as subcommand (SC-9 args-passthrough scoped to `auth login`; operators wanting generic `gh` composition use `pnpm devbox:shell` per SC-15-sibling read-only posture).
Iteration-env substrate verification (iter-237) ŌĆö Smoke 1: `bash -n packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh` ŌåÆ syntax valid (PASS).
Iteration-env substrate verification (iter-237) ŌĆö Smoke 2: `pnpm run 2>&1 | grep -E '^ +gh:auth$'` ŌåÆ 1 match (SC-3 pnpm wiring precedence honoured; `gh:auth` between `claude` and `prepare`).

requirements.title: Token persisted in named volume ŌĆö tokens stored at `/home/dev/.config/gh/` inside the Story 2.5 `keel_home_dev` named volume; no host bind-mount involved
substrate_evidence: Story 2.5 substrate (iter-188 landed; UNTOUCHED at Story 2.9 iter-237): `packages/devbox/docker-compose.yml` declares `keel_home_dev` named volume mounted at `/home/dev` with `INV-devbox-homedev-named-volume` enforcing non-toggle-able posture. `/home/dev/.config/gh/` inherits the named-volume persistence transitively ŌĆö token file (`hosts.yml`) lives inside the named volume by construction, not by Story 2.9 wrapper action. Story 2.9 does NOT create, mount, or modify any volume; composition-only on Story 2.5.

packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-237 NEW): Wrapper does NOT read, parse, inspect, bind-mount, or surface the token file. Zero `volume` / `mount` / `--mount` / `-v` flags in the final exec line (iter-239 `-e KEEL_DEVBOX_REPO_NAME=${REPO_NAME}` env-var addition is env-forward, not volume mount). Token file format + write location (`hosts.yml` under `/home/dev/.config/gh/`) owned by apt-installed upstream `gh` CLI (Dockerfile:123-136). SC-15 read-only posture codifies no-wrapper-token-access discipline.

Iteration-env substrate verification (iter-237) ŌĆö `grep -c 'volume\|mount\|\-v ' packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh` ŌåÆ 0 matches (verified post-iter-239: `-v ` as bind-mount flag absent; iter-239 `-e ` env-var is orthogonal). `grep -c '\.config/gh' packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh` ŌåÆ reference only in info-log literal (`token persists at /home/dev/.config/gh/`), not in any exec argument.

SC-12 scope carve-out (iter-235 pinned + iter-237 implementation-confirmed): Story 2.9 does NOT modify `packages/devbox/docker-compose.yml`. The stale `# TODO(Story 2.8 / 2.9): named-volume mounts for OAuth tokens.` at compose:156 is intentionally left in place per SC-12 ŌĆö Story 2.5 compose named-volume already subsumes the token-mount requirement for both Claude (`/home/dev/.claude/`) + `gh` (`/home/dev/.config/gh/`) token paths; TODO is self-documenting across Stories 2.8 + 2.9.

Live-smoke AC 2 runtime verification ŌĆö `pnpm gh:auth` ŌåÆ OAuth completes ŌåÆ `docker exec keel-devbox ls -la /home/dev/.config/gh/` shows `hosts.yml` token file owned by UID 1000 inside `keel_home_dev` named volume; `docker inspect --format '{{json .Mounts}}' keel-devbox | jq` confirms `/home/dev` mount source is the named volume NOT a bind-mount path ŌĆö deferred to M4-Pro operator workstation per ┬¦ Testing Standards ground-(c) + Story 2.5 iter-187 + Story 2.8 iter-230 precedent cluster.

Adversarial AC-2 coverage delegated to iter-CR: Blind Hunter verifies zero path-traversal or token-handling logic (SC-15); Edge Case Hunter probes Story 2.5 named-volume contract durability (operator explicit `-v /path:/home/dev` override not reachable via `pnpm gh:auth` surface per Story 2.6 wrapping; raw `docker run` is FR1 non-toggle-able invariant violation); Acceptance Auditor verifies AC 2 verbatim match ŌĆö tokens at `/home/dev/.config/gh/` + no host bind-mount involved.

requirements.title: Token survives restart ŌĆö `pnpm devbox:restart` or `pnpm devbox:stop && start` preserves token; Ralph's subsequent `gh pr view` / `git push` reuses existing token (no re-auth)
substrate_evidence: Story 2.5 substrate (iter-188 landed; UNTOUCHED at Story 2.9 iter-237): `keel_home_dev` named volume survives container restart by Docker semantics ŌĆö `docker compose down` without `--volumes` flag does NOT delete named volumes; `docker compose up -d` on existing volume mounts same volume. Story 2.6 `pnpm devbox:restart` + `pnpm devbox:stop && start` are canonical named-volume-safe restart primitives. Token-survival is property of Docker named-volume substrate, NOT of Story 2.9 wrapper. Mirrors Story 2.8 AC-3 inheritance.

packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-237 NEW): Wrapper is stateless ŌĆö re-invocation after restart hits same pre-flight branches + final exec line against same container. No wrapper-side state cache; `gh` reads token from mounted named volume on every invocation. Idempotent by construction.

Upstream `gh` CLI semantics (apt-installed; Dockerfile:123-136; Renovate `apt` manager-tracked per Story 1.15): On invocation, `gh` checks `/home/dev/.config/gh/hosts.yml` for existing token; valid ŌåÆ skip OAuth, proceed silently; missing/expired ŌåÆ auth-required error or re-auth flow (AC 4 path). Upstream GitHub-owned behavior ŌĆö Story 2.9 does NOT duplicate, proxy, or inspect.

Ralph usage-path semantics (AC-3-specific): AC 3 references `gh pr view` + `git push` (not `gh auth login`). These are Ralph pre-push gate + PR-monitoring usage paths (Epic 3 Story 3.7 consumer). Token-reuse-across-restart is transitive: (i) `gh` reads `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml` on invocation; (ii) `hosts.yml` in `keel_home_dev` named volume per AC 2; (iii) named volume survives `pnpm devbox:restart` per Story 2.5 + 2.6 substrate. Story 2.9 wrapper NOT on `gh pr view` / `git push` path ŌĆö those are Epic 3 Story 3.7 scope consuming persisted token without new wrapping.

Live-smoke AC 3 runtime verification ŌĆö sequence `pnpm gh:auth` (OAuth completes first run) ŌåÆ `pnpm devbox:restart` ŌåÆ `docker exec keel-devbox gh pr view <N>` second invocation ŌåÆ `gh` proceeds silently (no OAuth re-trigger; existing token reused) ŌĆö deferred to M4-Pro operator workstation per ┬¦ Testing Standards ground-(c).

Adversarial AC-3 coverage delegated to iter-CR: Blind Hunter verifies wrapper adds no re-auth-forcing logic (no `rm /home/dev/.config/gh/*` or `--clear-token` flag injection); Edge Case Hunter probes concurrent-invocation + token-file-corruption (upstream `gh` owns recovery ŌĆö AC 4 re-auth-pointer path activates); Acceptance Auditor verifies AC 3 verbatim match ŌĆö restart preserves token + subsequent `gh pr view` / `git push` reuses it.

requirements.title: Expired/revoked token surfaces clear pointer + Ralph pre-push gate halt-able ŌĆö `gh` invocations under expired/revoked token surface clear pointer to re-run `pnpm gh:auth`; Ralph's pre-push gate (Epic 3) treats this as a halt-able condition rather than silently retrying
substrate_evidence: packages/devbox/README.md (iter-237 MODIFIED): New `## gh CLI authentication (Story 2.9)` H2 sibling section (between Story 2.8 Claude H2 and `## cc-devbox upstream provenance` H2) documents re-auth path operator-facing ŌĆö quick-start + OAuth flow + args-composition recipes (`--web`, `--hostname github.com`, `--scopes "repo,workflow"`) + re-auth pointer + pre-push-gate halt-able cross-ref to Epic 3 Story 3.7. 67 lines added.

AGENTS.md (iter-237 MODIFIED): New `### gh CLI authentication (Story 2.9)` H3 sibling section (between Story 2.8 Claude H3 and top-level `## Ralph loop`) under ┬¦ Devbox iteration environment documents agent-facing re-auth pointer ŌĆö `Re-auth pointer:` bullet: "if Ralph's `gh push` / `gh pr view` / `gh pr checks` tooling reports a gh auth failure, queue `pnpm gh:auth` as a fix task (operator-interactive). Agents SHOULD NOT attempt automated re-auth ŌĆö OAuth requires a host browser." 11 lines added.

packages/devbox/scripts/gh-auth-host.sh (iter-237 NEW): Re-auth flows through SAME pre-flight branches + same final exec line as initial auth ŌĆö wrapper is idempotent. Upstream `gh` CLI detects expired/revoked token and surfaces auth-required error (or re-triggers OAuth under `gh auth login` re-invocation); wrapper surfaces URL + code to operator terminal identically to AC 1. No separate re-auth code path needed.

Upstream `gh` CLI semantics (apt-installed): Token expiry + revocation detection + auth-required error messaging + re-OAuth-trigger logic (under explicit `gh auth login` re-invocation) owned by upstream. Clear error message surfacing is upstream behavior; Story 2.9 wrapper neither catches nor massages `gh`'s stderr. SC-15 read-only posture.

Epic 3 Story 3.7 halt-write consumer contract (Story 2.9 pins; Epic 3 implements): AC 4 second element pins `CI_BLOCKED` halt-reason contract per `INV-ralph-halt-reason-enum` closed halt-reason enum (PRD FR14k; `docs/invariants/ralph-execute.md` ┬¦ Halt schema). When Ralph's pre-push gate (Story 3.7) detects auth-broken gh invocation, it writes halt sentinel `{"reason":"CI_BLOCKED","note":"gh not authed ŌĆö run 'pnpm gh:auth'"}`. Agents inheriting Epic 3 scope MUST NOT retry push silently or invent new halt reason (┬¦ Halt ┬¦ Autonomy guardrail). Story 2.9 pins contract verbatim in `AGENTS.md ┬¦ gh CLI authentication (Story 2.9)` bullet; Epic 3 Story 3.7 implements halt-write. `INV-ralph-halt-reason-enum` consumed-not-modified.

Egress substrate (Story 2.3/2.4 whitelist unchanged): `packages/devbox/whitelist/github.txt` contains 7 entries covering all GitHub OAuth + API + raw-content + LFS/release + GHCR + package-container paths ŌĆö `api.github.com`, `github.com`, `raw.githubusercontent.com`, `objects.githubusercontent.com`, `codeload.github.com`, `ghcr.io`, `pkg-containers.githubusercontent.com`. Story 2.9 does NOT modify whitelist fragments (SC-12-parallel); re-auth traffic flows through unchanged egress contract.

Live-smoke AC 4 runtime verification ŌĆö sequence `pnpm gh:auth` (OAuth completes) ŌåÆ artificially expire/revoke via GitHub UI OR wait for real expiry ŌåÆ `docker exec keel-devbox gh pr view <N>` ŌåÆ `gh` detects expiration, prints re-auth pointer, operator re-runs `pnpm gh:auth` to refresh ŌĆö deferred to M4-Pro operator workstation per ┬¦ Testing Standards ground-(c).

Live-smoke AC 4 Ralph halt-write branch requires Epic 3 Story 3.7 shipped (substrate-contract-only at Story 2.9). Smoke at Epic 3 landing: Ralph pre-push gate invocation against expired-token devbox ŌåÆ expects `CI_BLOCKED` halt sentinel with `"note":"gh not authed ŌĆö run 'pnpm gh:auth'"` at `$RALPH_BASE_DIR/halt`; ralph.py reads halt + stops loop.

Adversarial AC-4 coverage delegated to iter-CR: Blind Hunter verifies wrapper does not suppress/mangle `gh`'s stderr (no `2>/dev/null` or stderr redirection on final exec); Edge Case Hunter probes partial-expiry (refresh-token valid + access-token expired ŌĆö upstream `gh` owns refresh-flow; Story 2.9 transparent), revocation-from-github-side (upstream reports 401 + prompts re-auth; same surfaced-URL flow as AC 1), Epic-3-halt-contract wording (`CI_BLOCKED` reason in closed enum; `note` field free-form but agents MUST NOT invent new halt reasons per ┬¦ Halt ┬¦ Autonomy guardrail); Acceptance Auditor verifies AC 4 two-element match ŌĆö expired/revoked token ŌåÆ clear re-auth pointer to `pnpm gh:auth` + Epic 3 pre-push gate halts rather than silent-retries.
